title: ulmus americana var. rubra
intro: Ulmus americana var. rubra, also known as Red Elm, is a variety of the American Elm tree, characterized by its distinctive red inner bark. The Red Elm has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bark and leaves of the Red Elm have been used to make medicinal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. In addition to its medicinal properties, the Red Elm has been used to make herbal preparations, such as salves and creams, which are used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
